title: Support podman image trust command
body:
implemented:
```
$ podman image trust show --json
$ podman image trust show --raw
$ podman image trust show
$ podman image trust set --type <type> --pubkeysfile <filepath> <registryname>
```


Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@mtrmac Could you give this a quick review to make sure @QiWang19 is going in the right path. 
@QiWang19 Could you show the output of the commands you have now?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Missing man pages,
Missing command completions

@QiWang19 You need to gofmt your code
And fix the validate stuff.

I directly read the file as []byte and use struct， can I continue on this?
@mtrmac PTAL
Can we move API-relevant code out of `cmd/podman` into `libpod/image`? I think there's value in a reusable API to retrieve this info.
I only moved `func IsValidImageURI(imguri string) (bool, error)`  into `libpod/image/utils.go`

I really don't think `cmd/podman` is the place for this much code - we should either look into having policy generation functions in `libpod/image` that generate the files, and calling those from here, or moving the code to somewhere in `pkg/` (maybe `pkg/trust`?)
@mheon I added pkg/trust. how about now, still too much code?
@QiWang19 Two more things I think can afford to move, but looks a lot better now - I get a little worried when Github won't display a file by default because it's too long
@rhatdan No, I want to remove the WIP
LGTM other then some nits on the description. Fix those and we can merge.
